vectors.description: Valid mutual handshake with minimum permissions
Agent credential has expired — currentTimestamp >= expiryTimestamp fails LessThan(64)
Agent credential expiry equals currentTimestamp exactly — edge case: current == expiry is expired

expected.failsAt: AgentPolicy circuit: LessThan(64) constraint notExpired.out === 1 (equality fails strict less-than)

vectors.description: Agent credential valid with currentTimestamp one second before expiry
Agent permissions don't satisfy required scope — bit 1 required but not set
Required scope exceeds permissions on all bits — 0x00 perms vs 0xFF required

expected.failsAt: AgentPolicy circuit: requiredBits[i] * (1 - permBits[i]) !== 0 for i in [0..7]

vectors.description: Zero permission bitmask with zero required scope — vacuously valid (no permissions needed)
EdDSA signature does not verify against claimed public key — tampered R component
EdDSA signature valid but over wrong message — message hash mismatch

expected.failsAt: HumanAuth circuit: EdDSAVerifier message input mismatch

vectors.description: Agent Merkle proof verifies against a root no longer in agentRootExists — root rotated
Merkle root was valid 2 epochs ago but has been rotated out of active set
Valid Merkle inclusion proof at maximum tree depth (depth 20)
Merkle proof depth 21 exceeds circuit capacity (MAX_DEPTH = 20)

expected.failsAt: Circuit compilation: MerkleInclusionProof template parameter DEPTH > MAX_DEPTH

vectors.description: Merkle proof with incorrect sibling at level 5 — computed root mismatches
Valid single-hop delegation narrowing permissions from 0xFF to 0x0F
Valid delegation with identical scope (no narrowing, but still valid subset)
Delegatee has exactly one extra bit (bit 7) not in delegator scope
Delegatee attempts to gain permissions delegator doesn't have — bit 1 escalation
Delegatee attempts to extend expiry beyond delegator — LessEqThan(64) fails
Delegatee expiry equals delegator expiry exactly — valid (LessEqThan allows equality)
Permission bit 4 set without bit 3 — 0x15 = 0b10101 has bit 4 and bit 2 but not bit 3
Permission bit 3 set without bit 2 — 0x09 = 0b01001 has bit 3 and bit 0 but not bit 2
All cumulative bits properly chained — 0x1F = 0b11111 (bits 0-4 all set)
Zero bitmask is valid — no cumulative constraints apply (vacuously true)
Valid 3-hop delegation chain with narrowing at each step
4th delegation hop rejected by on-chain hop counter (MAX_DELEGATION_HOPS = 3)
Scope escalation occurs at hop 2 in a 3-hop chain
Second handshake with same nonce rejected by on-chain usedNonces mapping
Same nonce used by different agent — still rejected (nonces are global, not per-agent)

expected.note: Nonces are global to prevent cross-agent replay

vectors.description: Delegation proof submitted with a nonce already consumed in a prior delegation
Delegation attempted without prior handshake for this sessionNonce
Delegation proof's previousScopeCommitment does not match on-chain state
Delegation to non-enrolled agent — delegateeMerkleRoot not in agentRootExists

expected.note: CIP-1: delegatee Merkle inclusion proof prevents phantom delegatees

vectors.description: Permission bitmask equals BN254 field modulus p — exceeds field, rejected before bit decomposition

expected.failsAt: AgentPolicy circuit: bitmask >= p causes undefined behavior in Num2Bits
expected.note: BN254 field modulus: values >= p are not valid field elements

vectors.description: Agent expiry timestamp of zero — always expired regardless of current time
Agent expiry at max uint64 — never expires within LessThan(64) domain
Two different secrets produce different nullifiers — collision resistance of Poseidon
v0.3 — Two-hop delegation chain narrowing 0xFF -> 0x0F -> 0x03; each hop is a proper subset
v0.3 — Delegation token signed by attacker key but verified against delegator's pubkey must fail (Delegation.circom EdDSA sig check)
v0.3 — Same delegator/delegatee with different sessionNonce must produce different delegationNullifier (replay protection)
v0.3 — previousScopeCommitment binds to delegator identity: Poseidon3(delegatorScope, delegatorCredCommitment, delegatorExpiry) must be deterministic
v0.3 — delegationToken = Poseidon4(previousScopeCommitment, delegateeCommitment, delegateeScope, delegateeExpiry) — deterministic and binds all 4 fields
v0.3 — newScopeCommitment = Poseidon3(delegateeScope, delegateeCredCommitment, delegateeExpiry) — chain seed for next hop, identity-bound
v0.3 — LeanIMT edge case: single-leaf agentTree root equals the leaf itself (length=0 path, all-zero siblings)
v0.3 — LeanIMT two-leaf agentTree root equals Poseidon2(leaf0, leaf1) when delegatee is leaf at index=1

v0.3 — Delegation Groth16 proof exposes exactly 6 public signals in canonical order: [newScopeCommitment, delegationNullifier, delegateeMerkleRoot, previousScopeCommitment, sessionNonce, currentTimestamp]
v0.3 — Drop FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED (bit 4) while keeping FINANCIAL_MEDIUM+SMALL (bits 2+3): delegator 0x1C -> delegatee 0x0C — valid subset and cumulative-encoding still holds

v0.3 — Even though delegator scope 0x1C (bits 2+3+4) is cumulative-valid, narrowing to 0x14 (bits 2+4 — missing bit 3) violates cumulative invariant on delegatee and the Delegation circuit must reject

expected.failsAt: Delegation circuit: cumulative bit invariant on delegateeScope (bit4 implies bit3, bit3 implies bit2)

vectors.description: Valid SD-JWT allow() + present() round-trip — issuer signs, holder presents, verifier accepts
SD-JWT receipt past exp timestamp — verifier must reject with token_expired
SD-JWT presented to wrong audience — aud claim mismatch must cause rejection
SD-JWT issued without nonce in production mode — must fail; nonce is required to prevent replay

expected.failsAt: bolyra.sd_jwt.allow: nonce required in production mode

vectors.description: SD-JWT presented with a nonce that was already consumed — replay must be rejected

expected.failsAt: bolyra.sd_jwt.verify: nonce already present in consumed_nonces set

vectors.description: SD-JWT payment receipt with amount exceeding the permission cap — must fail FINANCIAL_SMALL limit
SD-JWT selective disclosure — action claim revealed, amount claim hidden from verifier
Two separately issued SD-JWT receipts must have distinct jti values — JTI uniqueness invariant
Valid proof envelope for a handshake — content-type set, proof bytes well-formed, all required fields present
Proof envelope without proof_type field — structural validation must reject it
Proof envelope with invalid base64 in proof_bytes_b64 — decoder must reject before ZK verification

expected.failsAt: bolyra.proof_envelope.decode: base64 decode error on proof_bytes_b64

vectors.description: Proof envelope with extra unknown fields — forward-compat: unknown fields preserved, envelope not rejected
Proof envelope containing both handshake and delegation proofs in one payload — cross-circuit aggregation accepted
Proof envelope with empty public_signals array — at least one public signal required, must fail

expected.failsAt: bolyra.proof_envelope.validate: public_signals must contain at least one element

vectors.description: Valid session token issued after a successful handshake — JWT well-formed, nullifier claim present, not expired
Session token past exp timestamp — verifier must reject with session_expired
Delegation narrows scope from 0x0F to 0x03 and session token reflects the narrowed scope — valid
Session token without nullifier claim — identity binding absent, must be rejected

expected.failsAt: bolyra.session_token.verify: nullifier claim is required for identity binding

vectors.description: Stale session nonce reused in a second token request — replay must be rejected
IO-contract: valid agent-only bundle, request covered by the signed binding, fresh nonce — the spawned verifier returns allow
IO-contract: same valid bundle under --nonce-mode host — the verifier returns allow and defers nonce burning to the host via consume_nonces
IO-contract: truncated JSON on stdin — the verifier fails closed with deny malformed_input at exit 0
IO-contract: capability requires WRITE_DATA but the proof-anchored scope is only READ_DATA — deny scope_exceeded
IO-contract: request.model does not match the model the proof commits to — deny model_mismatch

Contract §3.3/§3.5: a verdict with no `kind` field is interpreted as the `zk` class for backward compatibility with wire-version-1 verifiers that predate the field (e.g. the reference `bolyra verify`). Validated against the §3.4 verdict schema, no CLI spawn.

Contract §3.5: a `zk`-class verifier MAY set `kind` explicitly; `"kind":"zk"` is equivalent to omitting it for a revision-aware host (a strict pre-revision host rejects any kind-bearing verdict per §11). The §3.4 schema accepts the field on an allow verdict.

Contract §3.5: a `classical`-class verifier (Bolyra Core — ES256K receipts, JWT delegation, policy/nonce checks) returns the same allow envelope tagged with `kind: classical`. Exercises the classical class at the §3.4 schema level.

Contract §3.5/§9: a third-party (`external`-class) verifier denies using the shared denial-code registry and tags the verdict `kind: external`. Exercises the external class plus a deny verdict carrying `kind`.

Contract §3.5: `kind` MUST be one of classical|zk|external. A verdict carrying an out-of-enum `kind` MUST be rejected by the §3.4 verdict schema (the host fails closed on the malformed verdict, §7.2). Conformance = schema rejection.

expected.reason: kind value outside the classical|zk|external enum must be rejected by the §3.4 verdict schema

vectors.description: Host conformance positive control (§16.3): a well-behaved verifier returns a bare `allow` at exit 0 and the host-under-test MUST relay `allow`. Proves the host-conformance suite is not vacuously always-deny.

Host conformance positive control (§16.3): a well-behaved verifier returns a schema-valid `deny code=expired` at exit 0. The host MUST relay the deny with the verifier's code and MUST NOT attach a host failure_class — this is the verifier's decision, not a fail-closed override.
Offered case (1) malformed stdout (§5.2/§7.2): the verifier writes non-JSON to stdout at exit 0. The host MUST fail closed.

Offered case (1) malformed stdout, single-object framing (§5.2/§7.2): the verifier writes TWO concatenated JSON verdicts. A lenient parser that reads only the first object is forbidden; the host MUST fail closed.
Offered case (1) malformed stdout, verdict schema (§3.4/§7.2): the verifier writes valid JSON whose `verdict` is neither allow nor deny. The host MUST fail closed.
Offered case (1) malformed stdout, verdict schema (§3.3/§3.4/§7.2): a `deny` verdict missing the REQUIRED `code`/`message`. The host MUST fail closed.

Offered case (1) malformed stdout, single-object parse (§5.2/§7.2): a valid `allow` verdict followed by trailing non-JSON bytes. Trailing bytes after the one object are a fail-closed condition; the host MUST NOT act on the leading allow.
Offered case (2) timeout / no output (§6/§7.2): the verifier reads stdin, writes nothing, and never exits. The host owns the timeout; it MUST kill the process and deny.
Offered case (2) timeout / no output (§6/§7.2): the verifier writes a partial JSON verdict then hangs forever. No complete object arrives and the process never exits; the host timeout MUST fire.

Offered case (3) nonzero exit (§7.1/§7.2/§16.4): the verifier prints a syntactically valid `allow` and then exits NON-ZERO. Exit status is part of the contract; the host MUST treat this as deny and MUST NOT act on the stdout allow.

Offered case (3) killed-by-signal (§7.2): the verifier terminates via an unsolicited fatal signal before producing a verdict. The host MUST treat this as deny. The process both dies by signal AND leaves stdout empty — both are §7.2 fail-closed conditions — so a conforming host may report either `signal_death` (checks termination first, like the reference host) or `unparseable_stdout` (checks the empty stdout first).
Offered case (4) oversize output (§6/§7.2): the verifier floods stdout past the host's output bound. The host MUST bound the read, kill the process, and deny — it MUST NOT buffer unboundedly.

Offered case (5) reserve-before-act (§7.3/§16.5): in host nonce mode the verifier returns `allow` with `consume_nonces`. With an empty durable store the host MUST reserve every nonce THEN allow. Observable: decision=allow AND the nonce is durably present in the store afterwards.

Offered case (5) reserve-before-act, replay (§7.3/§16.5): the same allow+consume_nonces verdict, but the nonce is ALREADY in the durable store. The host MUST reject as replay even though the verifier returned allow — proving reservation happens before the host acts on the verdict.

Offered case (5) reserve EVERY entry (§7.3/§16.5): an allow carrying TWO nonces where only the second is already reserved. Because ANY conflict is a replay, the host MUST deny — proving it reserves the whole list, not just the first entry.

Offered case (1) malformed stdout, closed verdict schema (§3.4/§7.2): an `allow` carrying a disallowed additional property. The §3.4 schema is closed (additionalProperties:false); the host MUST fail closed rather than ignore the unknown field.

Offered case (1) malformed stdout, verdict `kind` enum (§3.5/§7.2): an `allow` with a `kind` outside classical|zk|external. §7.2 requires the host to treat an unrecognized `kind` as a malformed verdict and fail closed.

Offered cases (1)+(5), verdict schema (§3.2/§3.4/§7.2): an `allow` with an EMPTY `consume_nonces` array. The schema requires minItems:1 (the key is omitted when there is nothing to burn); the host MUST fail closed.

Offered cases (1)+(5), verdict schema (§3.2/§3.4/§7.2): an `allow` whose `consume_nonces` entry omits the REQUIRED `issuer_key` and `retain_until`. The host MUST validate the full closed schema and fail closed — it MUST NOT reserve a partial nonce entry.

Offered case (1) malformed stdout, closed deny schema (§3.4/§7.2): a `deny` verdict carrying a disallowed additional property. The deny shape is closed; the host MUST fail closed rather than relay an unrecognized field.

Offered cases (1)+(5), closed consume_nonces entry schema (§3.2/§3.4/§7.2): an `allow` whose nonce entry carries a disallowed additional property. The host MUST validate each entry as a closed object and fail closed — it MUST NOT reserve an entry with unrecognized fields.

Offered cases (1)+(5), consume_nonces entry field types (§3.2/§3.4/§7.2): an `allow` whose nonce entry has a string `retain_until` instead of an integer. The host MUST validate entry field types and fail closed.
